title: Data, Database Systems, and the Query Journey · Query Repair Bay
summary: A support organization has inconsistent spreadsheets, duplicated customer facts, and no defensible retention process. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a database adoption and data-lifecycle decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.
Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug support system has 3 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.

Inspect the Data Lifecycle trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Lifecycle, keep Data Lifecycle connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: For Data Lifecycle, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Lifecycle, name the row grain, Data Lifecycle precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Lifecycle, trace this concrete input and predict rows or changes before running it: A debug support system has 3 spreadsheets, repeated customer identifiers, conflicting ticket status values, and no destruction date. Trace one data item through its lifecycle and the query-processing components before selecting a storage boundary.
For Inspect the failure record · Debug Data Lifecycle, challenge the documented misconception with result, constraint, or plan evidence: Keeping every field forever is the safest data policy.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Data Custodianship
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-data-custodianship-1ae0df" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; database-system and data-lifecycle evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Data Custodianship.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Data Custodianship into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Data Custodianship, keep Data Custodianship connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-data-custodianship-1ae0df
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN
SELECT ticket_id, status FROM tickets WHERE status = 'open';
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
